diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: Diagnosis was made based on histopathological findings of nodules in the skin, which revealed intradermal histiocytic proliferation with mononuclear cells and no foam cells. The patient's clinical presentation included a red rash, nodules on various parts of the body, muscle aching, stiffness, and swelling of the right wrist. Laboratory tests showed positive antinuclear antibody and rheumatoid factor, and skin biopsy results supported the diagnosis of MRH.
